sections.one_line: How Kernos separates shared tools from per-member state, credentials, and aggregation
Core modules and wiring included in this primitive
Member service credential storage and encryption key management
External service definitions, fields, and auth/channel rules for Kernos service bindings
Tool descriptor schema and metadata for tool permissions and routing
Checks tool code for hardcoded paths, IDs, and secret env reads before registration
Tool invocation context for member, instance, space, storage, and credentials
Invocation-time checks that enforce tool integrity, authority, credentials, and sandboxing
Audit logging for tool invocations, category fields, and SHA-256 payload digests
How tools fit existing credentials, covenants, and standing orders through the agent layer
Future hooks for stock integrations, tool visibility, cookie import, and member aggregation
